assertions.text: Correctly identifies the system as high-risk via Annex I Path A (Art. 6(1)) because Regulation 2017/745 (Medical Devices) is listed in Annex I Section A
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly states 'HIGH-RISK under Art. 6(1) — the Annex I safety component path — STEP 4: RISK TIER DETERMINATION — HIGH-RISK VIA ART. 6(1), ANNEX I PATH' and cites 'Regulation 2017/745 is explicitly listed in Annex I Section A, Item 11'

assertions.text: Explains that MDR CE marking does NOT automatically satisfy AI Act compliance requirements
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly states 'Does the CE mark cover you? No — but it is integrated, not replaced.' and lists the additional AI Act-specific requirements not covered by MDR CE marking

assertions.text: Explains that AI Act requirements (Arts. 9-17) must be integrated into the existing MDR conformity assessment procedure
assertions.evidence: Response states 'Article 43(3) integrates the MDR notified body procedure into the AI Act conformity pathway' with specific reference to the integration mechanism

assertions.text: States the 2 August 2027 deadline specifically for Annex I safety component systems
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly states 'Annex I safety component obligations apply from 2 August 2027 (one year later than Annex III systems)'

assertions.text: Identifies hospital deployer obligations under Art. 26 (instructions compliance, staff competence, monitoring, log retention)
assertions.evidence: Response details 'Hospital's deployer obligations (Art. 26): Staff training against automation bias, 6-month log retention, continuous monitoring and incident reporting to the vendor and market surveillance authority, GDPR DPIA, FRIA for public bodies'
